[Atlanta] A love letter to The Varsity — <p>Some may call The Varsity a tourist trap, its food a gut bomb. Sure, you likely won’t spot it on any list of best Atlanta eateries, and the Michelin crowd probably won’t be found bellying up to th

Atlanta Magazinemainstream_independent

e counter for a peach pie. But we locals consider it part of the fabric of our city. An Atlanta institution.</p> <p>The post <a rel="nofollow" href="https://www.atlantamagazine.com/news-culture-articles/a-love-letter-to-the-varsity/">A love letter to The Varsi

city-atlantanewsroom-atlanta-magazine

[Atlanta] A writer returns to Pawleys Island in search of a way to begin life anew — <p>I crest the Waccamaw River Bridge on an August evening when a storm is looming in the distance. I haven’t been back to Pawleys Island in nearly a decade

Atlanta Magazinemainstream_independent

, but the smell of the Georgetown paper mills and the rhythmic thunk of the bridge’s seams under my tires call up the years I spent visiting this tiny spit of South Carolina Lowcountry. My father, the poet James Dickey, loved what he called the “unbroken openn

city-atlantanewsroom-atlanta-magazine

[Atlanta] Atlanta’s World Cup schedule is here — <p>This summer, Atlanta hosts five group-stage games, two knockout matches, and a semifinal. Watch top-ranked Spain (and its most famous player, Lamine Yamal) take on rising star Cabo Verde i

Atlanta Magazinemainstream_independent

n the opening match. On June 21, Spain will take on Saudi Arabia in a clash of titans. Get ready for a month of excitement!</p> <p>The post <a rel="nofollow" href="https://www.atlantamagazine.com/news-culture-articles/atlantas-world-cup-schedule-is-here/">Atla

city-atlantanewsroom-atlanta-magazine

[Atlanta] Behind the scenes of Atlanta’s 5 year bid to become a World Cup host city — <p>In 2017, Atlanta was one of more than 40 candidate cities in North America competing to host FIFA World Cup 2026™. Each candidate had to offer a lot to

Atlanta Magazinemainstream_independent

be considered. It all culminated in a 2022 nationally televised, hour-long broadcast from New York announcing the 16 winners.</p> <p>The post <a rel="nofollow" href="https://www.atlantamagazine.com/news-culture-articles/behind-the-scenes-of-atlantas-5-year-bi

city-atlantanewsroom-atlanta-magazine

[Atlanta] Remembering Bob Horner, the former Braves slugger who became an Atlanta icon — <p>Ted Turner, Atlanta’s beloved, inimitable “Captain Outrageous” and “the Mouth from the South,” died on May 6. The visionary CNN founder, Atlanta Bra

Atlanta Magazinemainstream_independent

ves owner, environmentalist, philanthropist, yachtsman, humanitarian, restaurateur, 1977 America’s Cup winner and Time magazine’s 1991 Man of the Year was 87.</p> <p>The post <a rel="nofollow" href="https://www.atlantamagazine.com/news-culture-articles/remembe
